perturbation.original_text: 4. Independent Contractor and Limited Agency You will be deemed to be an independent contractor, and will have no authority to act as agent for either Nationwide or the Funds in any matter or in any respect. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Nationwide, on behalf of each Fund, appoints you as each Fund's agent for the limited purpose of accepting orders for purchase, redemption and exchange of Shares, and receipt of such orders by you therefore shall constitute receipt by the applicable Fund of such orders for purposes of determining the price per Share at which such orders will be executed, in accordance with Rule 22c-1 under the 1940 Act. Except as specifically set forth herein, nothing in this Agreement will be deemed or construed to make you a partner, employee, representative or agent of Nationwide or any Fund or to create a partnership, joint venture, syndicate or association between you and us.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Independent Contractor and General Agency You will be deemed to be an independent contractor, and will have no authority to act as agent for either Nationwide or the Funds in any matter or in any respect. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Nationwide, on behalf of each Fund, appoints you as each Fund's agent for all purposes outlined within this agreement, including accepting orders for purchase, redemption and exchange of Shares, and receipt of such orders by you therefore shall constitute receipt by the applicable Fund of such orders for purposes of determining the price per Share at which such orders will be executed, in accordance with Rule 22c-1 under the 1940 Act. Except as specifically set forth herein, nothing in this Agreement will be deemed or construed to make you a partner, employee, representative or agent of Nationwide or any Fund or to create a partnership, joint venture, syndicate or association between you and us.
perturbation.explanation: The title change to "General Agency" from "Limited Agency" alongside the modified text expands the agent's role to "all purposes outlined within this agreement." This directly contradicts the initial statement that the Servicing Agent has "no authority to act as agent for either Nationwide or the Funds in any matter or in any respect." This creates a contradiction as the agreement now states the Servicing Agent both has and does not have general agency authority.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Independent Contractor and General Agency You will be deemed to be an independent contractor, and will have no authority to act as agent for either Nationwide or the Funds in any matter or in any respect. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Nationwide, on behalf of each Fund, appoints you as each Fund's agent for all purposes outlined within this agreement, including accepting orders for purchase, redemption and exchange of Shares, and receipt of such orders by you therefore shall constitute receipt by the applicable Fund of such orders for purposes of determining the price per Share at which such orders will be executed, in accordance with Rule 22c-1 under the 1940 Act. Except as specifically set forth herein, nothing in this Agreement will be deemed or construed to make you a partner, employee, representative or agent of Nationwide or any Fund or to create a partnership, joint venture, syndicate or association between you and us.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the agency relationship from "Limited Agency" to "General Agency," stating the agent now has authority for "all purposes outlined within this agreement." This directly contradicts the initial statement in both the changed text and the contradicted text, which asserts that the agent has "no authority to act as agent for either Nationwide or the Funds in any matter or in any respect." This creates a clear and irreconcilable contradiction, as the agent is simultaneously granted and denied general agency authority, leading to ambiguity and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 15. Privacy Program Each party to this Agreement agrees to protect Customer Information (defined below) and to comply as may be necessary with requirements of the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act, the relevant state and federal regulations pursuant thereto, including Regulation S-P, and state privacy laws (all the foregoing referred to as "Privacy Law"). 4 Customer Information means any information contained on an application of a customer ("Customer") of a Fund or other form and all nonpublic personal information about a Customer that a party receives from the other party. Customer Information includes, by way of example and not limitation, name, address, telephone number, social security number, date of birth and personal financial information. The parties shall establish and maintain safeguards against the unauthorized access, destruction, loss or alteration of Customer Information in their control, which are no less rigorous than those maintained by a party for its own information of a similar nature. In the event of any improper disclosure of any Customer Information, the party responsible for the disclosure will immediately notify the other party. The Servicing Agent represents that it has implemented and maintains a comprehensive written information security program that contains appropriate security measures to safeguard Customer Information that the Servicing Agent receives, stores, maintains, processes or otherwise accesses in connection with the provision of services hereunder, including protecting such Customer Information against cyber attack. The provisions of this Privacy Program section shall survive the termination of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. Privacy Program Each party to this Agreement agrees to protect Customer Information (defined below) and to comply as may be necessary with requirements of the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act, the relevant state and federal regulations pursuant thereto, including Regulation S-P, and state privacy laws (all the foregoing referred to as "Privacy Law"). 4 Customer Information means any information contained on an application of a customer ("Customer") of a Fund or other form and all public information about a Customer that a party receives from the other party. Customer Information includes, by way of example and not limitation, name, address, telephone number, social security number, date of birth and personal financial information. The parties shall establish and maintain safeguards against the unauthorized access, destruction, loss or alteration of Customer Information in their control, which are no less rigorous than those maintained by a party for its own information of a similar nature. In the event of any improper disclosure of any Customer Information, the party responsible for the disclosure will immediately notify the other party. The Servicing Agent represents that it has implemented and maintains a comprehensive written information security program that contains appropriate security measures to safeguard Customer Information that the Servicing Agent receives, stores, maintains, processes or otherwise accesses in connection with the provision of services hereunder, including protecting such Customer Information against cyber attack. The provisions of this Privacy Program section shall survive the termination of the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of Customer Information to include "all public information", it contradicts the initial sentence stating it should comply with the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act and Regulation S-P, which focus on protecting *nonpublic* personal information. The original text defines Customer Information as 'nonpublic personal information', but the changed text redefines it to include 'all public information.' This creates an in-text contradiction regarding what constitutes Customer Information and what needs to be protected under the Privacy Program.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15. Privacy Program Each party to this Agreement agrees to protect Customer Information (defined below) and to comply as may be necessary with requirements of the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act, the relevant state and federal regulations pursuant thereto, including Regulation S-P, and state privacy laws (all the foregoing referred to as "Privacy Law"). 4 Customer Information means any information contained on an application of a customer ("Customer") of a Fund or other form and all public information about a Customer that a party receives from the other party. Customer Information includes, by way of example and not limitation, name, address, telephone number, social security number, date of birth and personal financial information. The parties shall establish and maintain safeguards against the unauthorized access, destruction, loss or alteration of Customer Information in their control, which are no less rigorous than those maintained by a party for its own information of a similar nature. In the event of any improper disclosure of any Customer Information, the party responsible for the disclosure will immediately notify the other party. The Servicing Agent represents that it has implemented and maintains a comprehensive written information security program that contains appropriate security measures to safeguard Customer Information that the Servicing Agent receives, stores, maintains, processes or otherwise accesses in connection with the provision of services hereunder, including protecting such Customer Information against cyber attack. The provisions of this Privacy Program section shall survive the termination of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines "Customer Information" to include "all public information", which directly contradicts the first sentence of the same section requiring compliance with Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act and Regulation S-P. These regulations focus on protecting *nonpublic* personal information, creating a strong in-text contradiction regarding the scope of protected Customer Information. The contradicted text is the same section where the change was introduced.

perturbation.original_text: 18.2 Agreement to Restrict Trading. You agree to promptly and fully cooperate with any reasonable request made by the Fund to address market timing or excessive trading strategies identified by Nationwide in accordance with the applicable provisions of Rule 22c-2 and agree to restrict or prohibit further purchases or exchanges of Shares by a Shareholder that has been identified by such Fund as having engaged in transactions of the Fund's Shares (directly or indirectly through your account) that violate policies established by the Fund for the purpose of eliminating or reducing any dilution of the value of the outstanding Shares issued by the Fund.
perturbation.changed_text: 18.2 Agreement to Restrict Trading. You agree to promptly and fully cooperate with any reasonable request made by the Fund to address market timing or excessive trading strategies identified by Nationwide in accordance with the applicable provisions of Rule 22c-2; however, you retain the right to decide, at your sole discretion, whether to restrict or prohibit further purchases or exchanges of Shares by a Shareholder that has been identified by such Fund as having engaged in transactions of the Fund's Shares (directly or indirectly through your account) that violate policies established by the Fund for the purpose of eliminating or reducing any dilution of the value of the outstanding Shares issued by the Fund.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'however, you retain the right to decide, at your sole discretion' introduces an internal contradiction. The original text explicitly states that you 'agree to restrict or prohibit' trading, implying an obligation. The modified text gives you discretionary power over whether to restrict trading, negating the original obligation. It is contradicting the original agreement to 'agree to restrict or prohibit' which makes it not a mandatory provision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 18.2 Agreement to Restrict Trading. You agree to promptly and fully cooperate with any reasonable request made by the Fund to address market timing or excessive trading strategies identified by Nationwide in accordance with the applicable provisions of Rule 22c-2; however, you retain the right to decide, at your sole discretion, whether to restrict or prohibit further purchases or exchanges of Shares by a Shareholder that has been identified by such Fund as having engaged in transactions of the Fund's Shares (directly or indirectly through your account) that violate policies established by the Fund for the purpose of eliminating or reducing any dilution of the value of the outstanding Shares issued by the Fund.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'however, you retain the right to decide, at your sole discretion,' which directly contradicts the original agreement to 'agree to restrict or prohibit.' This introduces a discretionary element where the original text implied a mandatory obligation, creating an in-text contradiction as defined in the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition where a clause is placed in multiple locations (in this case, the same location but with a conflicting implication due to the added discretionary clause). This contradiction makes the obligation unclear and potentially unenforceable.
